evals.name: cwv-render-blocking-and-lcp
evals.prompt: Lighthouse on https://example.com/landing flagged two issues: 'Eliminate render-blocking resources' (3 sync stylesheets in head, 2 sync scripts before content) and 'LCP > 4 s on mobile' (LCP element is the hero image, currently a 480 KB JPEG). The site is Next.js 16 App Router. Fix both.
evals.expected_output: Per-issue fix blocks for render-blocking and LCP. Render-blocking fix shows critical CSS inlining + async load + next/script with strategy. LCP fix uses next/image with priority + fetchPriority='high' + preload, with explicit guidance not to set loading='lazy'. Each fix has a verification step.
expectations: The response loads references/cwv.md, not the other reference files
The render-blocking fix mentions inline critical CSS and an async-load pattern with rel='preload' + onload
The render-blocking fix specifically uses next/script with a strategy prop (lazyOnload, afterInteractive, or worker)
The LCP fix uses Next.js <Image> with priority and fetchPriority='high'
The LCP fix explicitly warns against loading='lazy' on the LCP image
Each issue is presented in the per-issue output block format defined in SKILL.md (Issue / Diagnosed cause / Files changed / Fix applied / Verification / Expected impact)
The summary line at the end reports 'Fixed 2 of 2' or equivalent

evals.name: on-page-titles-meta-h1
evals.prompt: I ran Screaming Frog on https://acme.com and got these issues: 47 pages have duplicate <title> tags (all say 'Acme Inc'), 31 pages have no <meta name='description'>, and 12 pages have multiple <h1> elements. The site is Astro. Generate a plan to fix these without touching unrelated files.
evals.expected_output: Per-issue fix blocks for titles, meta descriptions, H1s. Title fix gives a templating pattern (e.g., {pageTitle} | {Brand}). Meta description fix shows Astro layout pattern. H1 fix explains the one-H1-per-page rule and how to demote extras. Each fix references on-page.md, not the others.
expectations: The response loads references/on-page.md
The title fix proposes a per-page templated title pattern (not a single hardcoded string)
The title fix mentions the 50-60 character target
The meta description fix mentions the 150-160 character target
The meta description fix shows where to put the meta in an Astro layout
The H1 fix explicitly states one H1 per page and explains how to demote the extras to h2/h3
The fix output format matches the per-issue block defined in SKILL.md
The skill does NOT also fix unrelated SEO categories the user did not ask about

evals.name: technical-robots-sitemap-canonical
evals.prompt: GSC says my sitemap.xml returns 404, my robots.txt has 'Disallow: /' (oops), and my product pages are missing <link rel='canonical'>. The site is plain HTML hand-built, deployed on Vercel. Fix all three.
evals.expected_output: Three fix blocks. robots.txt rewritten to allow crawl with sitemap directive. Sitemap created (either /public/sitemap.xml hand-rolled OR app/sitemap.ts if migrating to Next, but plain HTML implies hand-rolled). Canonical tag added per page with self-referencing absolute HTTPS URL.
expectations: The response loads references/technical.md
The robots.txt fix removes 'Disallow: /' and adds 'Sitemap: ...' directive
The robots.txt fix preserves Allow rules for /*.css$ and /*.js$ so Googlebot can render
The sitemap fix produces valid XML with <urlset>, <url>, <loc>, <lastmod>
The canonical fix uses absolute HTTPS URLs (not relative or http://)
The canonical fix recommends self-referencing canonicals
The skill does NOT propose moving to Next.js / a framework just to fix these (the user said plain HTML)

evals.name: geo-ai-crawlers-and-llms-txt
evals.prompt: Our SEO scan flagged that GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, and Google-Extended are all blocked in our robots.txt, and we have no llms.txt. We're a B2B SaaS at saas.example.com that wants to show up in ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Claude. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Two fix blocks. robots.txt updated to allow GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, and Google-Extended (with discussion of the trade-off — Google-Extended controls AI training, OAI-SearchBot vs GPTBot, etc.). llms.txt created at site root with the standard structure (H1, blockquote one-liner, About paragraph, Docs / Data / Policies sections).
expectations: The response loads references/geo.md
The robots.txt fix unblocks GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, and Google-Extended
The fix mentions or references the AI crawler matrix (OAI-SearchBot, ChatGPT-User, CCBot, etc.)
The fix explains that Google-Extended affects AI Overview eligibility, not Google Search rankings
The llms.txt fix produces a Markdown file with H1 + blockquote one-liner + About paragraph + sectioned link list
The llms.txt fix recommends Content-Type: text/markdown
Verification step includes curling with each AI user agent and checking 200 responses
Skill does NOT recommend blocking any AI crawler (since user explicitly wants AI visibility)

evals.name: mixed-multi-domain-audit
evals.prompt: Here are all the failing checks from PageSpeed Insights + my SEO crawler on https://shop.example.com (Next.js, Vercel): LCP 5.2s on mobile, missing meta descriptions on 18 product pages, sitemap.xml has stale lastmod dates from 2023, GPTBot blocked in robots.txt. Solve them.
evals.expected_output: Four per-issue blocks routed across all four references. LCP fix from cwv.md, meta description fix from on-page.md, sitemap fix from technical.md (with Next.js app/sitemap.ts pattern), GPTBot fix from geo.md. Final summary line.
expectations: The response loads at least 3 of the 4 references (cwv.md, on-page.md, technical.md, geo.md) — exactly the ones needed
The LCP fix uses Next.js <Image> with priority
The meta description fix uses the Next.js metadata export pattern
The sitemap fix uses app/sitemap.ts with MetadataRoute.Sitemap and dynamic lastModified
The GPTBot fix unblocks GPTBot in robots.txt (or app/robots.ts)
Each issue is in its own per-issue output block
The final summary line reports 'Fixed 4 of 4' or notes which were skipped with reasons

evals.name: ambiguous-fix-with-tradeoff-flagged
evals.prompt: PageSpeed Insights flagged 'Reduce thin content' on 200+ tag archive pages (each has 1-2 posts, ~50 words). Site is WordPress. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes this is a judgment call — bulking up tag pages with filler content is an anti-pattern. It should either (a) recommend noindexing low-value tag pages OR (b) flag this as needing human judgment per the 'When to stop and ask' section in SKILL.md.
expectations: The response loads references/on-page.md
The skill does NOT recommend bulk-generating filler content for the tag pages
The skill either recommends noindexing low-value tag pages, or surfaces this as a judgment call requiring human input
If a judgment call is surfaced, the question is concrete (e.g., 'do these tags have SEO value or are they admin-only?')
The skill mentions the WordPress-specific path to noindex (via Yoast or Rank Math plugin, or a hook)
